title: John Ternus to become Apple CEO
summary:
Apple announced a significant leadership change, with John Ternus, Senior Vice President of Hardware Engineering, appointed as the next Chief Executive Officer, effective September 1, 2026. Current CEO Tim Cook will transition to Executive Chairman of Apple’s board of directors, a move unanimously approved by the board after long-term succession planning. Arthur Levinson will become Lead Independent Director, and Ternus will join the board.

Tim Cook, who will facilitate a smooth transition, expressed profound gratitude for his time as CEO, commending Ternus as a visionary engineer with integrity, perfectly positioned to lead Apple forward. Ternus, having spent nearly his entire career at Apple and mentored by Steve Jobs and Tim Cook, conveyed his deep appreciation and optimism, vowing to uphold the company’s defining values.

Cook's 15-year CEO tenure was marked by extraordinary growth, seeing Apple’s market capitalization rise from $350 billion to $4 trillion and revenue nearly quadrupling. He oversaw the introduction of new product categories like Apple Watch, AirPods, and Apple Vision Pro, grew Services into a $100 billion business, and spearheaded the transition to Apple-designed silicon. Cook also solidified Apple's commitment to core values, reducing its carbon footprint, prioritizing privacy, and enhancing accessibility.

Ternus, who joined Apple in 2001, became Senior VP of Hardware Engineering in 2021. He has been pivotal in developing groundbreaking products across iPhone, Mac, iPad, and Apple Watch, including the MacBook Neo and the iPhone 17 lineup. His leadership has driven innovations in product reliability, durability, and sustainable materials, such as recycled aluminum and 3D printed titanium.
chinese_title: 约翰·特纳斯将成为苹果首席执行官
chinese_summary:
苹果宣布了一项重大的领导层变动，硬件工程高级副总裁约翰·特努斯（John Ternus）被任命为下一任首席执行官，任期自2026年9月1日起生效。现任首席执行官蒂姆·库克（Tim Cook）将转任苹果董事会执行主席，此举是在长期继任计划后由董事会一致批准的。阿瑟·莱文森（Arthur Levinson）将担任首席独立董事，特努斯也将加入董事会。

蒂姆·库克将协助顺利过渡，他对担任首席执行官期间表达了深切感谢，并称赞特努斯是一位富有远见且正直的工程师，完全有能力带领苹果向前发展。特努斯职业生涯的大部分时间都在苹果度过，并受到史蒂夫·乔布斯和蒂姆·库克的指导。他表达了深切的谢意和乐观情绪，誓言将维护公司的核心价值观。

库克在15年的首席执行官任期内，苹果实现了非凡的增长，市值从3500亿美元增至4万亿美元，营收增长近四倍。他监管了Apple Watch、AirPods和Apple Vision Pro等新产品类别的推出，将服务业务发展成千亿美元规模，并主导了向苹果自研芯片的转型。库克还巩固了苹果对核心价值观的承诺，包括减少碳足迹、优先保护隐私和增强无障碍功能。

特努斯于2001年加入苹果，并于2021年成为硬件工程高级副总裁。他在iPhone、Mac、iPad和Apple Watch等多个产品线的开创性产品开发中发挥了关键作用，其中包括MacBook Neo和iPhone 17系列。在他的领导下，产品在可靠性、耐用性和可持续材料方面实现了创新，例如回收铝和3D打印钛。

title: Kimi K2.6: Advancing open-source coding
summary:
The Kimi K2.6 model is now open-sourced, representing a significant advancement in open-source coding. It offers state-of-the-art coding, long-horizon execution, and enhanced agent swarm capabilities, accessible through Kimi.com, the App, API, and Kimi Code.

K2.6 shows substantial improvements in long-horizon coding, generalizing across various languages (Rust, Go, Python) and tasks. Noteworthy examples include optimizing model inference in Zig for a 20% speed increase over LM Studio and autonomously overhauling an 8-year-old financial engine, yielding a 185% throughput leap. Enterprise feedback consistently praises its surgical precision, intelligent problem-solving, and stable performance in complex engineering tasks, with internal benchmarks showing significant gains over Kimi K2.5.

Beyond pure coding, K2.6 excels in "Coding-Driven Design," converting prompts into full front-end interfaces and supporting simple full-stack workflows. Its "Agent Swarm" capabilities have been qualitatively improved, scaling to 300 sub-agents executing 4,000 coordinated steps simultaneously, significantly reducing latency and expanding operational scope. It can also transform documents into reusable "Skills."

K2.6 powers "Proactive Agents" for continuous, autonomous operations across applications, demonstrating enhanced reliability and safety awareness. The "Claw Groups" research preview introduces an open, heterogeneous ecosystem where K2.6 coordinates diverse agents and humans as true collaborators.

Benchmarks confirm Kimi K2.6's strong performance, often rivaling or exceeding leading closed-source models and marking a clear improvement over Kimi K2.5 across agentic, coding, and reasoning evaluations.

title: Atlassian enables default data collection to train AI
summary:
Atlassian has enabled default data collection from its cloud products to train its artificial intelligence (AI) models, a move that has sparked privacy concerns among users. The company will use data from products like Jira and Confluence to enhance AI-powered features, including "Atlassian Intelligence," which offers capabilities like summarization and content generation within their platforms.

While Atlassian states that sensitive data, such as private content from sandboxes and certain admin-controlled fields, will be excluded, the broad scope of default collection raises questions for organizations handling sensitive information or those operating under strict data privacy regulations like GDPR. The company emphasizes its commitment to privacy and security, noting that data will be de-identified and used in aggregate to prevent individual identification.

Users retain the option to opt out of this data collection. However, the default "opt-in" setting shifts the responsibility to individual users and administrators to actively manage their privacy preferences, rather than requiring explicit consent. This strategy aligns with a growing trend among tech companies leveraging vast user data to accelerate AI development, often prioritizing innovation over user-centric privacy defaults. The article highlights the ongoing tension between technological advancement and data privacy in the AI era.
